channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How much power does the Fed chair really have?
item.description: On paper, the Fed chair is just one vote among many. In practice, the job carries far more influence. We analyze what gives the Fed chair power.

item.title: An ape, a tea party â and the ability to imagine
item.description: The ability to imagine â to play pretend â has long been thought to be unique to humans. A new study suggests one of our closest living relatives can ...

item.title: American Ben Ogden wins silver, breaking 50 year medal drought for U.S. men's cross-country skiing
item.description: Ben Ogden of Vermont skied powerfully, finishing just behind Johannes Hoesflot Klaebo of Norway. It was the first Olympic medal for a U.S. men's cross...

item.title: DHS faces funding deadline. And, courts fast-track Somali asylum seeker hearings
item.description: Congress has until Friday to reach a deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security. And, several asylum cases filed by Somali migrants in immigrati...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Buddhist monks' 108-day Walk for Peace set to end in Washington DC
item.description: Nineteen monks have walked single file from Texas to Washington DC, silently advocating for more peace in the US.

item.title: Airlines adjust as Trump's policies dry up Cuba's jet fuel supplies
item.description: A Canadian airline suspends flights to Cuba as U.S sanctions and Trump's tariff threats force Havana to warn carriers there's no way to refuel on the ...

item.title: Trumpâs immigration chiefs are set to testify in Congress following protester deaths
item.description: The heads of agencies enforcing President Donald Trumpâs immigration agenda are set to testify before Congress.

item.title: Trump threatens to block opening of US-Canada bridge
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Tuesday he had a "positive" talk with Trump following the Gordie Howe International Bridge threat.

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration's effort to obtain Michigan voter data
item.description: The Trump administration has filed lawsuits against 24 states in an effort to obtain their voter rolls.

item.title: US lawmakers accuse justice department of 'inappropriately' redacting Epstein files
item.description: Republican Thomas Massie and Democrat Ro Khanna say the DOJ is not complying with their transparency law.

item.title: âWe hold these truths': Viewing the Declaration of Independence, visitors reflect on America at 250
Ben Ogden becomes 1st U.S. man in 50 years to medal in cross-country
item.description: Ben Ogden of Team USA won the silver medal in the cross-country sprint Tuesday at the Winter Olympics in Italy.

item.title: Marc Anthony says the way Beckham feud has played out is 'hardly the truth'
item.description: The 57-year-old performed at Brooklyn and Nicola Peltz Beckham's wedding reception in 2022.

item.title: How much gold is in an Olympic gold medal, and how much is it worth?
item.description: Olympic medals have what's known as a "melt value." But they're worth far more financially than their mineral contents, an auction expert notes.

item.title: See the Olympic medal count for the 2026 Winter Games
item.description: Here is a look at where the medal count stands for Team USA and other nations as the competition heats up at the 2026 Winter Olympics.

item.title: As a fellow Puerto Rican, Bad Bunny's Super Bowl show was personal
item.description: While the performance carried a message of unity for Latinos in general, the artist sang for the island we are both from, writes BBC Mundo's Ronald Ãv...

item.title: How Americansâ optimism about their future has changed, according to new polling
